recipes.title: Pierogi
recipes.description: Polish dumplings with tender dough wrapped around fillings of potato-cheese, meat or sauerkraut and mushroom.
steps: Knead flour, warm water, egg and salt into a soft, elastic dough; rest covered 30 minutes.
Boil potatoes until tender; mash dry.
Sauté one finely chopped onion in butter until golden; mix with potato, twaróg, salt and pepper.
Roll dough thin and cut 7cm circles.
Place a teaspoon of filling on each, fold and press edges to seal firmly.
Drop pierogi into boiling salted water in batches; cook 3 minutes after they float.
Pan-fry remaining onion in butter until golden.
Drain pierogi and toss with onion butter; serve with sour cream.

recipes.title: Bigos
recipes.description: Polish hunter's stew of sauerkraut, fresh cabbage, mixed meats and sausage simmered for hours.
steps: Soak dried mushrooms in 300ml hot water 30 minutes.
Render diced bacon, then brown cubed pork and beef in the fat.
Add chopped onions and cook until soft.
Add drained sauerkraut, shredded fresh cabbage, mushrooms with their liquid, bay, juniper, tomato paste and wine.
Add 500ml water and simmer covered 2 hours, stirring occasionally.
Slice kiełbasa and add with prunes; simmer 30 more minutes.
Adjust seasoning. Best when reheated the next day.

recipes.title: Żurek
recipes.description: Polish sour rye soup with sausage, eggs and a dollop of horseradish.
steps: Render diced bacon in a soup pot; add chopped onion and sliced sausage.
Add water, sliced carrot, bay and marjoram; simmer 30 minutes.
Add diced potatoes; cook 15 minutes until tender.
Stir the rye starter well and pour into the soup; simmer 10 minutes.
Crush garlic and stir in; do not boil hard.
Temper sour cream with hot soup, then stir back in.
Hard-boil eggs, halve, and place in bowls before ladling soup over.

recipes.title: Kotlet Schabowy
recipes.description: Polish breaded pork cutlet pan-fried golden, served with potatoes and sauerkraut.
steps: Pound chops between cling film to 1cm thick; season with salt and pepper.
Set up flour, beaten eggs and breadcrumbs in three plates.
Dredge chops in flour, dip in egg, then coat fully in breadcrumbs.
Heat oil and butter in a wide pan until hot.
Fry cutlets 4 minutes per side until deep golden and cooked through.
Drain briefly on paper and serve hot with potatoes.

recipes.title: Gołąbki
recipes.description: Polish cabbage rolls stuffed with pork and rice, simmered in tomato sauce.
steps: Core cabbage and blanch whole 5 minutes; peel off softened leaves.
Simmer rice 8 minutes (half-cooked); drain and cool.
Sauté chopped onion in oil until soft; cool.
Combine pork, rice, onion, egg, crushed garlic, salt and marjoram.
Place 2 tablespoons filling on each leaf, fold sides and roll tightly.
Line a pot with leftover leaves; pack rolls seam-down in tight layers.
Pour passata and broth over; cover and simmer 90 minutes on low.
Spoon sauce over rolls to serve.

recipes.title: Barszcz
recipes.description: Polish beetroot soup with deep ruby colour, served clear or with meat dumplings.
steps: Peel and grate beetroots coarsely.
Combine beetroot, chopped onion, carrot, celery, bay, allspice and broth.
Bring to a simmer and cook 45 minutes over low heat.
Strain through a fine sieve, pressing gently; discard solids.
Return broth to pot; stir in crushed garlic, lemon juice, sugar and salt.
Warm through (do not boil) and serve clear or with small dumplings.

recipes.title: Pyzy
recipes.description: Polish potato dumplings with a pillowy, slightly chewy bite, served with bacon and onion.
steps: Boil potatoes in their skins until tender; peel and rice while warm.
Spread potato to cool slightly, then mound on a board and divide into quarters.
Replace one quarter with potato starch; add egg and salt.
Knead briefly until a soft dough forms; do not overwork.
Shape into walnut-sized balls.
Cook in batches in boiling salted water 5-7 minutes after they float.
Render diced bacon with chopped onion in butter until golden.
Drain dumplings and toss with bacon-onion mixture.

recipes.title: Placki Ziemniaczane
recipes.description: Crispy Polish potato pancakes, golden outside and tender inside.
steps: Grate potatoes and onion finely.
Place in a sieve and squeeze hard to remove as much liquid as possible.
Mix with eggs, flour, salt and pepper.
Heat oil in a wide frying pan over medium-high heat.
Drop heaped tablespoons of batter into oil and flatten lightly.
Fry 3-4 minutes per side until deep golden and crisp.
Drain on paper and serve hot with sour cream.

recipes.title: Rosół
recipes.description: Clear Polish chicken soup with carrots, root vegetables and thin egg noodles.
steps: Char unpeeled onion over a flame until blackened; this gives the soup its colour.
Place chicken, beef and onion in a large pot with 3L cold water.
Bring slowly to a simmer, skimming foam carefully for 20 minutes.
Add carrots, parsnip, celery root, leek, bay, allspice and salt.
Simmer uncovered on the lowest heat 2.5 hours; do not boil hard.
Strain broth into a clean pot; reserve carrots and shred some chicken.
Cook noodles separately; place in bowls, ladle broth, add carrots and chicken.
Top with chopped parsley.

recipes.title: Kopytka
recipes.description: Polish potato dumplings cut on a diagonal, with a chewy bite and a buttery finish.
steps: Boil potatoes in their skins until tender; peel and rice while warm.
Cool potatoes slightly, then mound on a board.
Add flour, egg and salt; knead briefly into a soft dough.
Roll into long ropes about 2cm thick.
Cut on a sharp diagonal into 2cm pieces.
Cook in boiling salted water 2-3 minutes after they float.
Toast breadcrumbs in butter until golden.
Drain dumplings and toss with the breadcrumb butter.

recipes.title: Naleśniki
recipes.description: Thin Polish crepes filled with sweet cheese, fruit or savoury fillings.
steps: Whisk flour, eggs, milk, sparkling water, sugar and salt to a smooth thin batter; rest 15 minutes.
Mash twaróg with sugar, vanilla, an egg yolk and raisins.
Heat a small frying pan and brush with butter.
Pour a thin layer of batter, swirl, and cook 1 minute until edges lift.
Flip and cook 30 seconds; stack between paper.
Place a spoonful of cheese filling on each crepe and roll into a parcel.
Pan-fry briefly in butter or serve with sour cream and jam.

recipes.title: Sernik
recipes.description: Polish baked cheesecake made with twaróg, eggs and a touch of vanilla.
steps: Press shortcrust pastry into a 24cm springform tin and chill.
Twice through a meat grinder or fine sieve, pass the twaróg until silky.
Cream butter with sugar until pale; beat in egg yolks one at a time.
Mix in twaróg, vanilla, potato starch and zest.
Whisk egg whites to firm peaks and fold into the mixture with raisins.
Pour onto the pastry base and smooth the top.
Bake at 160°C for 70 minutes until just set with a slight wobble.
Cool fully in the tin before slicing.
